---
title: "Perfect Observational data"
author: "Oamen Dominion, 2021332339"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: word_document
---
```{r eraser}
rm(list = ls())
cat("\014")
```
```{r true model coefficients}
set.seed(123)
# ---- True Model Coefficients ----
beta0 <- 650000
beta_size <- 5000
beta_reno <- 150000
beta_urban <- 200000
beta_dist <- -2000
beta_interaction <- 2000
```
```{r Create Perfect Orthogonal grid}
# use sum contrast to preserve orthogonality
options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.sum"))
# ---- Create Fully Crossed Orthogonal Design ----
design <- expand.grid(
  size_cat = c("Small", "Medium", "Large"),
  dist_cat = c("Near", "Far"),
  renovated = c("No", "Yes"),
  location = c("Rural", "Urban")
)
# Replicate each cell 90 times
design <- design[rep(1:nrow(design), each = 90), ]
rownames(design) <- NULL
# Verify dimensions and balance
dim(design)
table(design$size_cat, design$dist_cat)
table(design$renovated, design$location)
```
```{r Add Continuous Variables}
n <- nrow(design)

# FIX: Generate continuous variables WITHIN each cell of the factorial design
# so they are balanced (and thus orthogonal) across all factor combinations.
# Strategy: assign a centred sequence within every unique cell, then
# stack them in the same order as the replicated design rows.

# Number of cells and replicates per cell
n_cells <- nrow(expand.grid(
  size_cat = c("Small", "Medium", "Large"),
  dist_cat = c("Near", "Far"),
  renovated = c("No", "Yes"),
  location = c("Rural", "Urban")
))  # = 24
reps_per_cell <- 90

# Within-cell centred sequence (same for every cell → balanced across factors)
within_seq <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = reps_per_cell)  # sums to 0

# size: independent within-cell sequence (identical pattern, different variable)
design$size    <- rep(within_seq, times = n_cells)

# dist_km: Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization against size
# Start with a random base variable, then subtract the projection onto
# within_seq (size) — this guarantees exact zero linear correlation with size
# while producing a realistic-looking continuous variable (not a parabola)
set.seed(7)
base_dist <- rnorm(reps_per_cell)

# Remove the component of base_dist that overlaps with within_seq
base_dist_orth <- base_dist - (sum(base_dist * within_seq) /
                                sum(within_seq * within_seq)) * within_seq

# Mean-centre to ensure orthogonality with intercept
base_dist_orth <- scale(base_dist_orth, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)[,1]

# Replicate identically across all cells (preserves orthogonality with factors)
design$dist_km <- rep(base_dist_orth, times = n_cells)

# Mean-center globally to guarantee orthogonality with the intercept
design$size    <- scale(design$size,   center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
design$dist_km <- scale(design$dist_km, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)

# Sum-coded binary variables (-1 / +1) — orthogonal to intercept by construction
design$renovated_num <- ifelse(design$renovated == "Yes",  1, -1)
design$location_num  <- ifelse(design$location  == "Urban", 1, -1)

# Check
summary(design$size)
summary(design$dist_km)
```
```{r Response Variable}
# ---- Simulate Response Variable ----

# Step 1: Compute the deterministic signal first (no error)
signal <- beta0 +
          beta_size * design$size +
          beta_reno * (design$renovated == "Yes") +
          beta_urban * (design$location == "Urban") +
          beta_dist * design$dist_km +
          beta_interaction * (design$size * (design$location == "Urban"))

# Step 2: Derive sigma that forces R² = 0.35
# R² = Var(signal) / (Var(signal) + sigma²)
# => sigma² = Var(signal) * (1 - R²) / R²
target_r2 <- 0.35
var_signal <- var(signal)
sigma      <- sqrt(var_signal * (1 - target_r2) / target_r2)
cat("Derived sigma:", round(sigma, 2), "\n")

# Step 3: Add noise with the derived sigma
epsilon      <- rnorm(nrow(design), mean = 0, sd = sigma)
design$price <- signal + epsilon

# Check
summary(design$price)
head(design$price)
```
```{r Orthogonal matrix}
X <- model.matrix(~ size_cat + dist_cat + renovated + location, data = design)
t(X) %*% X
```
```{r Correlation Matrix}
# ---- Correlation Matrix ----
design_numeric <- data.frame(
  size     = design$size,
  dist_km  = design$dist_km,
  renovated = design$renovated_num,
  location  = design$location_num
)
# Correlation matrix — all off-diagonals should now be exactly 0
round(cor(design_numeric), 10)
```
```{r Correlation Scatterplot Matrix}
# ---- Continuous variables only in pairs plot ----
pairs(~ size + dist_km,
      data = design_numeric,
      main = "Scatterplot Matrix - Continuous Variables",
      col = as.numeric(design$location),
      pch = 19,
      cex = 0.5)
legend("topright",
       legend = c("Rural", "Urban"),
       col = c(1, 2),
       pch = 19)
```
```{r Linear Regression Model}
model <- lm(price ~ size + dist_km + renovated + location + size:location,
            data = design)
summary(model)
```
```{r Diagnostic Plots}
# ---- Diagnostic Plots ----
par(mar = c(4, 4, 2, 2))
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```
```{r Partial Residual Plots}
# ---- Partial Residual Plots via termplot() ----
# crPlots() does not support interaction terms; termplot() handles them cleanly.
par(mar = c(4, 4, 2, 2))
termplot(model,
         partial.resid = TRUE,
         smooth        = panel.smooth,   # loess smoother overlay
         col.res       = "grey50",
         col.smth      = "red",
         pch           = 19,
         cex           = 0.4,
         ask           = FALSE)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```
```{r export dataset}
# Export only the correctly coded columns needed for Python analysis
# Uses renovated_num and location_num (-1/+1 sum coded) instead of raw strings
# dist_km is the mean-centred quadratic version from the orthogonal design
export_data <- data.frame(
  size_cat     = design$size_cat,
  dist_cat     = design$dist_cat,
  renovated    = design$renovated_num,   # sum coded: No=-1, Yes=1
  location     = design$location_num,   # sum coded: Rural=-1, Urban=1
  size         = design$size,
  dist_km      = design$dist_km,
  price        = design$price
)

# Verify before export
cat("dist_km mean:", round(mean(export_data$dist_km), 10), "\n")
cat("dist_km range:", round(range(export_data$dist_km), 4), "\n")
cat("renovated unique:", unique(export_data$renovated), "\n")
cat("location unique:", unique(export_data$location), "\n")

write.csv(export_data, file = "C:/Users/MC/Downloads/hdata_experimental.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
cat("Dataset exported successfully!")
cat("\nRows:", nrow(export_data))
cat("\nColumns:", ncol(export_data))
```
